segments.text: Lesson 31.

translations.zh-Hans: 第31课。

segments.text: A Lovable Eccentric.

translations.zh-Hans: 可爱的怪人。

segments.text: Listen to the tape, then answer the question below.

translations.zh-Hans: 听录音，然后回答以下问题。

segments.text: Why did the shop assistant refuse to serve Dickie?

translations.zh-Hans: 为什么店员拒绝接待迪基？

segments.text: True eccentrics never deliberately set out to draw attention to themselves.

translations.zh-Hans: 真正的怪人从不故意引人注目。

segments.text: They disregard social conventions without being conscious that they are doing anything extraordinary.

translations.zh-Hans: 他们无视社会习俗，却没有意识到自己的行为有什么不同寻常。

segments.text: This invariably wins them the love and respect of others, for they add color to the dull routine of everyday life.

translations.zh-Hans: 这总能为他们赢得他人的喜爱和尊敬，因为他们给单调的日常生活增添了色彩。

segments.text: Up to the time of his death, Richard Colson was one of the most notable figures in our town.

translations.zh-Hans: 理查德·科尔森去世前，一直是我们镇上最引人注目的人物之一。

segments.text: He was a shrewd and wealthy businessman, but most people in the town hardly knew anything about this side of his life.

translations.zh-Hans: 他是一位精明而富有的商人，但镇上大多数人对他生活中的这一面几乎一无所知。

segments.text: He was known to us all as Dickie, and his eccentricity had become legendary long before he died.

translations.zh-Hans: 我们都叫他迪基，早在他去世之前，他的怪癖就已经成了传奇。

segments.text: Dickie disliked snobs intensely.

translations.zh-Hans: 迪基极其厌恶势利小人。

segments.text: Though he owned a large car, he hardly ever used it, always to go on foot.

translations.zh-Hans: 尽管他有一辆大汽车，却几乎从来不用，总是步行。

segments.text: Even when it was raining heavily, he refused to carry an umbrella.

translations.zh-Hans: 即使下着倾盆大雨，他也拒绝打伞。

segments.text: One day he walked into an expensive shop after having been caught in a particularly heavy shower.

translations.zh-Hans: 一天，他刚淋过一场罕见的暴雨，便走进一家高档商店。

segments.text: He wanted to buy a 300-pound watch for his wife, but he was in such a bedraggled condition that an assistant refused to serve him.

translations.zh-Hans: 他想给妻子买一块价值300英镑的手表，但他当时浑身湿透、狼狈不堪，一名店员因此拒绝接待他。

segments.text: Dickie left the shop without a word, and returned carrying a large cloth bag.

translations.zh-Hans: 迪基一言不发地离开商店，回来时提着一个大布袋。

segments.text: As it was extremely heavy, he dumped it on the counter.

translations.zh-Hans: 袋子非常沉，他把它重重地放在柜台上。

segments.text: The assistant asked him to leave, but Dickie paid no attention to him, and requested to see the manager.

translations.zh-Hans: 店员叫他离开，迪基却不予理会，并要求见经理。

segments.text: Recognizing who the customer was, the manager was most apologetic, and reprimanded the assistant severely.

translations.zh-Hans: 经理认出这位顾客后连连道歉，并严厉斥责了店员。

segments.text: When Dickie was given the watch, he presented the assistant with the cloth bag.

translations.zh-Hans: 迪基拿到手表后，把布袋交给了那名店员。

segments.text: It contained three hundred pounds in pennies.

translations.zh-Hans: 里面装着价值300英镑的一便士硬币。

segments.text: He insisted on the assistance counting the money before he left.

translations.zh-Hans: 他坚持要店员在他离开前把钱数清。

segments.text: Thirty thousand pennies in all.

translations.zh-Hans: 总共三万枚一便士硬币。

segments.text: On another occasion he invited a number of important critics to see his private collection of modern paintings.

translations.zh-Hans: 还有一次，他邀请了几位重要评论家来欣赏他私人收藏的现代画。

segments.text: This exhibition received a great deal of attention in the press, for though the pictures were supposed to be the work of famous artists, they had, in fact, been painted by Dickie.

translations.zh-Hans: 这次画展受到新闻界的广泛关注，因为那些据称出自名家之手的画，其实全是迪基自己画的。

segments.text: It took him four years to stage this elaborate joke simply to prove that critics do not always know what they are talking about.

translations.zh-Hans: 为了证明评论家并非总知道自己在说什么，他花了四年时间精心策划这场复杂的恶作剧。
